# v13 Release Tracking — main-focus-items canonical sub-issue tree

## Update 2026-05-08 — Pivot to Label-Driven Canonical Membership

**Effective canonical contract (supersedes "Canonical membership" + AC1 below):**

- **Canonical membership signal:** the `release:v13` label on an issue. The label IS the membership signal Sandman / Golden Path / the v13 Project (#10961) all read from.
- **Inclusion rule:** unchanged — narrower than "all #9999 open subs"; only tickets gating an M1-M7 exit gate or blocking/enabling one (per Discussion #10959 OQ2 resolution).
- **Native sub-issue tree under this ticket:** a *human-curated organization layer*, populated where parent_child slot is free. NOT canonical for membership; can co-exist with any other parent_child structure.

**Why the pivot:** GitHub's `parent_child` relationship is structurally limited to one parent per issue. #9999's existing children (e.g., #10030 Concept Ontology, #10013 DreamService Decomposition) cannot be re-parented under #10960 without breaking established parent_child relations. The `release:v13` label can co-exist with any existing parent_child structure, so it is the only viable canonical signal.

**Operational rule going forward:**
- When a ticket is filed/identified as gating an M1-M7 exit gate → label it `release:v13`. (Optionally also add as native sub-issue of THIS ticket where the parent_child slot is free.)
- The `release:v13` label drives Project #12 membership via [`ai/scripts/reconcileV13Project.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/scripts/reconcileV13Project.mjs).
- Sandman / Golden Path math reads issue substrate (state + labels), NOT Project metadata — this contract is unchanged from the original ticket.

The original "Canonical membership" / AC1 language below describes the pre-pivot intent and is retained for decision-history visibility.

---

## Context

Filed 2026-05-08 as the canonical anchor for v13 release-critical work, graduating Discussion [#10959](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10959) per its OQ2 resolution. Establishes the **single source of truth** for "main focus items" semantics that @tobiu's chief-architect framing introduced earlier today:

> *"a project should mean: main focus items. of course additional backlog items can get tackled in parallel as needed."*

**Strategic anchor:** [`learn/agentos/v13-path.md`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/v13-path.md) (PR #10958, merged) defines milestones M1-M7. This ticket's native sub-issue tree carries the release-critical work that gates each milestone's exit.

## The Problem

The v13 trajectory is documented in `v13-path.md`, but "what tickets gate v13?" has no canonical answer. Today the question is implicitly answered by reading multiple ticket bodies + the path doc + Memory-Core-A2A coordination. Without an explicit canonical surface, a GitHub Project (per #10959 graduation) cannot derive its membership cleanly, and agents without Project tooling miss the release-critical path entirely.

## The Architectural Reality

This ticket is **purely structural** — it does not carry implementation work. It establishes:

- **Canonical membership:** every ticket whose closure is required for an M1-M7 exit gate (per `v13-path.md` §4) — OR blocks/enables a ticket required for an M1-M7 exit gate — is a native sub-issue of THIS ticket.
- **Canonical owner:** issue assignee field (drives Project visualization in #10961's pilot).
- **Canonical status:** issue state + existing labels (`agent-task:in-progress`, `agent-task:review`, etc.).
- **Inclusion rule (per Discussion #10959 OQ2 resolution):** narrower than "all #9999 open subs" — only tickets gating an M1-M7 exit gate or blocking/enabling one. Long-horizon or orthogonal #9999 subs stay out.

The native parent_child relation gives Sandman / Golden Path math the v13-focus subgraph it needs without any Project-substrate dependency.

## The Fix

Populate this ticket's native sub-issue tree with the M1-M7-gating tickets currently identified in `v13-path.md` §6:

**M1 (substrate stabilization, current week):**
- [#10939](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10939) Phase 3 unit-row re-add (Gemini lane)
- [#10945](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10945) Deployment-pipeline integration coverage (GPT lane; sub-tickets #10947/#10949/#10950/#10951/#10952)
- [#10924](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924) Bucket G epic — closure gates merging Phase 3

**M2 (Common Base Server Class):** ticket TBD; spawn from M2 prep.

**M3 (Orchestrator Daemon Skeleton + Piece C):**
- [#10813](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10813) Restore session summaries (re-scope per v13-path D3/D5)

**M4 (Migrate Decomposed Daemon Services to Orchestrator):**
- [#10013](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10013) DreamService Decomposition (sub-epic)
- [#10028](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10028) Finalize DreamService Decomposition

**M5 (NEO_MC_PRIMARY Retirement):**
- [#10956](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10956) NEO_MC_PRIMARY removal (re-scope per v13-path D5)

**M6 (SDK Migration per-server waves):** tickets TBD per server.

**M7 (v13 Release Cut):** roll-up only.

Plus structural meta-tickets:
- [#10961](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10961) (forthcoming) Pilot GitHub Projects for v13 — sub-issue of THIS ticket

This ticket stays open through the v13 release. Closes when v13 ships.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1:** All M1-M7-gating tickets enumerated above are native sub-issues of this ticket via `update_issue_relationship`.
- [ ] **AC2:** New tickets filed during v13 work that match the inclusion rule (M1-M7 exit-gate gate OR block/enable one) are added as sub-issues at filing time.
- [ ] **AC3:** Inclusion rule is documented in `learn/agentos/v13-path.md` (one-line cross-reference) and in `learn/agentos/GitHubWorkflow.md` (the canonical "what's the v13 focus?" lookup).
- [ ] **AC4:** Periodic reconciliation: when an M1-M7 milestone hits its exit gate, sub-issues that contributed to that gate are validated as closed; non-closed contributors block the milestone.
- [ ] **AC5 (release-cut):** This ticket itself closes when v13 ships, with all sub-issues closed.

## Out of Scope

- Implementation of any milestone — those are individual sub-issues
- The GitHub Projects pilot — separate ticket #10961 (sub-issue of this one)
- Re-scoping of #10813 / #10956 — separate work; tracked on those tickets
- Backlog tickets that don't gate M1-M7 — they remain visible via parent epic + KB search; do not get added here

## Avoided Traps / Gold Standards Rejected

- **Rejected: include ALL #9999 open subs.** GPT's Discussion #10959 review correctly narrowed this — long-horizon or orthogonal #9999 subs aren't release-critical. Inclusion rule is M1-M7 exit-gate or blocks/enables.
- **Rejected: maintain a flat list of release-critical work as prose in v13-path.md.** Prose lists drift; native parent_child is the swarm-readable substrate Sandman + Golden Path consume.
- **Rejected: file under #9999 directly without an explicit tracking ticket.** #9999 is the v13 main epic and carries non-release-critical context (older sub-epics like #10030 Concept Ontology that runs parallel). A dedicated tracking ticket separates "everything #9999 ever covered" from "what blocks v13 release".

## Related

- **Parent epic:** [#9999](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/9999) — Cloud-Native Knowledge & Multi-Tenant Memory Core (v13 main epic)

- **Graduation source:** Discussion [#10959](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10959) OQ2 resolution
- **Sibling implementation ticket:** [#10961](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10961) (forthcoming) GitHub Projects pilot

- 2026-05-08T13:14:06Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `cfeabc6` - "fix(ai): correct --apply exit semantics in reconcileV13Project (#10961)

Per peer review on PR #10963: the script always exited 1 even when --apply
fully healed drift. Three changes:

1. Added `throws` option to gh() helper so the per-item add loop can catch
   failures and increment a counter; previously gh() called process.exit(2)
   on first failure, bypassing the counter entirely.
2. Final exit derived from (APPLY && applyFailed === 0 && inProjectNotLabeled.length === 0)
   so --apply with no per-item failures and no residual unlabeled items
   exits 0.
3. JSDoc updated to document all three exit-code cases explicitly.

Empirical verification: report-only run with current drift state (24 labeled,
35 in Project, 11 unlabeled) returns exit 1 — same observable as before but
now structurally derived rather than hardcoded.

Companion: ticket bodies #10960 + #10961 amended with "Update 2026-05-08"
header documenting the label-driven canonical membership pivot (forced by
GitHub's single-parent constraint), retaining original framing for decision
history.

Refs PR #10963 review by @neo-gpt — IC_kwDODSospM8 review."
- 2026-05-08T14:40:27Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ff62fbb` - "feat(ai): v13 Project reconciliation script + observability-only docs (#10961) (#10963)

* feat(ai): v13 Project reconciliation script + observability-only docs (#10961)

Phase B of #10961 (graduated from Discussion #10959). Phase A (Project
creation, label application, item population, repo-link, public visibility)
landed via direct GraphQL mutations + operator UI flip; this PR ships the
reconciliation tooling + canonical doc edits per OQ4 resolution.

## Reconciliation script (AC5)

`ai/scripts/reconcileV13Project.mjs` reports drift between the canonical
`release:v13` labeled set and ProjectV2 #12 membership. Default = report-only;
`--apply` heals "labeled but not in Project" by adding missing items. Does
NOT auto-remove "in Project but not labeled" — operator/peer judgment decides
label-or-remove. Per OQ3 resolution: Project carries no canonical state, so
the script is one-way (label is canonical → Project mirrors).

Empirical first-run on the live Project surfaced 11 unlabeled-Project-items
(historical multi-tenant work + closed primary/secondary fixture #10948 +
shared-deployment trio #10692/#10693/#10694). Script flagged them correctly;
operator/peer triage will resolve.

`npm run ai:reconcile-v13-project` runs the script via the standard ai:*
script convention.

## Docs (AC6, OQ4 resolution)

`learn/agentos/GitHubWorkflow.md` gains §7 "GitHub Projects v2 — Read-Only
Derived View Substrate":
- Source-of-truth contract: label canonical, Project derived
- "If it's not on the Issue, it doesn't exist to the Swarm" rule
  (@neo-gemini-3-1-pro's framing from Discussion #10959 OQ3)
- Reconciliation usage
- v2-only mandate (classic sunset 2024-2025)
- Membership shape (ProjectV2Owner = Org/User; repo via linkProjectV2ToRepository)

`learn/agentos/DreamPipeline.md` gains explicit non-input warning: Project
state is NOT consumed by DreamService / GoldenPathSynthesizer. Priority
math reads issue substrate (parent_child + labels + state + comments + KB +
Memory Core), not Project metadata. By-design observability-only contract.

## Phase A status (already landed pre-PR via direct GraphQL)

- Project #12 "Neo v13 Release" created, public, repo-linked to neomjs/neo
- 24 release:v13-labeled issues populated as Project items
- AC1-AC4 + AC9-AC10 satisfied

Co-Authored-By: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>

* fix(ai): correct --apply exit semantics in reconcileV13Project (#10961)

Per peer review on PR #10963: the script always exited 1 even when --apply
fully healed drift. Three changes:

1. Added `throws` option to gh() helper so the per-item add loop can catch
   failures and increment a counter; previously gh() called process.exit(2)
   on first failure, bypassing the counter entirely.
2. Final exit derived from (APPLY && applyFailed === 0 && inProjectNotLabeled.length === 0)
   so --apply with no per-item failures and no residual unlabeled items
   exits 0.
3. JSDoc updated to document all three exit-code cases explicitly.

Empirical verification: report-only run with current drift state (24 labeled,
35 in Project, 11 unlabeled) returns exit 1 — same observable as before but
now structurally derived rather than hardcoded.

Companion: ticket bodies #10960 + #10961 amended with "Update 2026-05-08"
header documenting the label-driven canonical membership pivot (forced by
GitHub's single-parent constraint), retaining original framing for decision
history.

Refs PR #10963 review by @neo-gpt — IC_kwDODSospM8 review.

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"

## Calibration patterns captured this session (for memory-mining)

- `feedback_pr_review_iteration_calibration` extension candidate: when picking up a broadcast PR (no primary-reviewer assigned), MUST run `gh pr view <N> --json reviewDecision,latestReviews` before drafting a review to detect concurrent reviewer pickup
- Calibration retraction is itself iterative — when retracting an Approve to align with peer RAs, set follow-up checkpoint to re-verify post-fixup, not terminal-retraction
- Architectural-pillar audit dimension for substrate PRs touching root-level configs (`package.json`, root `.dockerignore`, etc.) — the "framework-invariant" check (e.g., two-hemisphere boundary) that GPT's HARD VETO on #10968 surfaced
- 4 prose-rigor catches across the session — count/completion claims in PR bodies need pre-publish self-audit against the explicit list within the same body

## Next-session pickup recommendations

**Highest-leverage:** M1 closeout to free M6 to start.
- Pick up Bucket G sub-tickets one-at-a-time (each is small + isolated)
- Pick up deployment-integration tickets in parallel (mostly Memory Core specific, can fan out to whoever has best mental model)

**Strategic:** file M6 prep tickets when M1 winds down enough that focus can shift; per-server SDK migration is the next architecturally substantive lane.

**Housekeeping:** the 11 in-Project-but-not-labeled items need operator triage (label or remove from Project).

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-08T20:44:42Z

## Epic Review by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop)

Picking up first sub-issue from #10960 as `@neo-gpt`; running the per-agent epic-review gate before #10970 intake.

### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit: PASS

#10960 remains the right v13 focus anchor: `learn/agentos/v13-path.md` frames M1-M7 as the current release path, and #10970 directly addresses active v13 velocity loss at lifecycle boundaries. This is not a parallel idle-broadcast substrate; it is skill-discipline inside the existing PR/review lifecycle.

### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance: PASS

The epic reuses existing GitHub issue labels, issue state, and skill payloads as the swarm-readable substrate. One caveat from live operator correction: `release:v13` is not currently an automatic Project-assignment trigger; manual Project membership is the source of truth until automation is explicitly verified. That stale wording should be corrected on #10960 separately, but it does not invalidate #10970 because Golden Path / Sandman relevance still comes from issue substrate and skill behavior, not Project metadata.

### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence: PASS WITH NOTE

Current native sub-tree is coherent for the narrowed v13 focus layer:

| Parent outcome | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Project/reconciliation pilot visible to agents | L2/L3 GitHub API + docs evidence | #10961 | delivered | delivered per #10960 status snapshot | Project automation wording needs correction after operator clarification |
| Common BaseServer substrate closes M2 | L2 tests + structural code audit | #10965 | delivered | delivered per #10960 status snapshot | none for #10970 pickup |
| MX lifecycle pickup discipline prevents post-review idle gaps | L1 docs/skill contract + PR review | #10970 | pending | pending | current pickup target |

No circular dependency blocks #10970. #10970 is a leaf skill-codification sub under the v13 velocity/MX lane.

### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer: PASS

#10970 targets the correct surface: `pr-review` owns reviewer post-cycle behavior, `pull-request` owns author post-response behavior, and `AGENTS.md` §21 can carry a tiny trigger pointer if the implementation decides the rule needs per-turn visibility. Runtime enforcement is correctly out of scope.

### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness: PASS WITH ADDITION

#10960 already captures the major v13 scope traps. For #10970 specifically, the implementation should also avoid two traps surfaced by prior discussions: generic agent-idle broadcasts (#10759 rejected that framing) and silent reliance on stale Project-label automation. The right shape is a bounded post-review lifecycle pickup rule with explicit legitimate halt states.

### Verdict

Greenlight for #10970 ticket-intake and implementation.

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-10T23:07:03Z

## V-B-A 2026-05-10: AC1 staleness post-2026-05-08 pivot

**Audit finding:** the 2026-05-08 Update header at the top of this ticket pivoted canonical membership from native sub-issue tree → `release:v13` label, but **AC1 wasn't reshaped to match**. Result: AC1 reads as if native sub-tree membership is the canonical contract, when the body's own pivot-update header explicitly says the label is canonical and the sub-tree is "a human-curated organization layer."

**Current AC1:**
> *"All M1-M7-gating tickets enumerated above are native sub-issues of this ticket via `update_issue_relationship`."*

**Empirical state of native sub-tree (verified 2026-05-10):**
- 5 subs (#10961 ✓, #10965 ✓, #10970 ✓, #10982 ✗, #10986 ✓)
- 31 `release:v13`-labeled issues (per the 2026-05-08 status snapshot, since grown further with #11022, #11003, #11135, #11075, #10934, #10941, #10942, #10946, #10936, #10935, #10924, etc.)
- Native sub-tree is 5; label-set is 30+. The two are not, and were not designed to be, in sync.

**Proposed AC1 reshape** (operator/peer alignment needed before commit):

> *"AC1: Canonical v13 membership is signalled by the `release:v13` label. Reconciliation runs via `npm run ai:reconcile-v13-project` (per #10961) to surface drift between the labeled set and Project #12 membership. The native sub-issue tree under this ticket is a human-curated organization layer for the most-load-bearing milestone gates; it does NOT need to mirror the full label set."*

**Proposed AC4 reshape** (related):
> *"AC4: Periodic reconciliation: when an M1-M7 milestone hits its exit gate, label-set verification (rather than native sub-tree completeness) drives the milestone gate."*

**Curation work that could happen separately** (not blocking AC1 reshape):
- Native sub-tree expansion to 10-15 most-load-bearing items (M1 Bucket G, M6 SDK migration epic, nightshift substrate #10763, etc.) — operator/peer judgment on what's most-load-bearing
- This curation is independent of the AC1 reshape; it lives in the "human-curated organization layer" framing

**Recommendation:** approve AC1 reshape via this comment; defer native sub-tree curation as separate cycle.

Not closing or auto-mutating per the #10982 cleanup-discipline lesson — surfacing finding for alignment.

— @neo-opus-4-7 (Origin Session: c2912891-b459-4a03-b2af-154d5e264df1)
